everyone.name: Savitha Raghunathan
everyone.bio: Savitha Raghunathan is a Senior Software Engineer at Red Hat, currently focusing on Application Modernization and Cloud Native adoption. Her role as a maintainer for the CNCF Sandbox project Konveyor has provided her with valuable insights into the growing demand for modernizing critical applications to align with cloud-native technologies, thereby deepening her understanding of the application lifecycle. In her previous position, Savitha worked as a platform engineer, where she dedicated herself to building internal developer platforms using Kubernetes and other cloud native projects.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Contributing to CNCF projects has been incredibly fulfilling, and the support and positivity I've experienced from the community during difficult periods, including the pandemic, have deepened my bond with this community. This role as a CNCF Ambassador has given me a chance to reciprocate that support, share my valuable experiences, and facilitate the onboarding of new contributors and adopters into the CNCF ecosystem.

everyone.name: Chad M. Crowell
everyone.bio: Chad is a Principal SRE at Akamai, and author of the book "Acing the Certified Kubernetes Administrator Exam". Chad has been an instructor for over six years on platforms such as A Cloud Guru (now Pluralsight) and INE. Through KubeSkills, he helps people find fun and interactive ways to learn Kubernetes, Linux, and containers.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I really enjoy helping people get involved in open source software. I have realized the power of community, in lifting others up and succeeding together, and I love to share it with others. I believe it is my duty in life to share what I've learned in order to make intellectual progress as a human race.

everyone.name: Gerardo Lopez
everyone.bio: Gerardo López Falcón is a CNCF Ambassador, Docker Captain, and Google Developer Expert, with a focus on cloud-native technologies, Kubernetes, and DevOps practices. He leads Cloud Native Costa Rica and frequently hosts meetups to empower the local tech community. As a dedicated advocate for open-source, he has co-authored resources like "Kubernetes Observability in Action" and shares insights on his blog, where he covers best practices and deep dives into tools like Docker, Prometheus, and Cilium.

everyone.name: Andrzej (Andy) Kozlowski
everyone.bio: I’m a Lead/Senior Cloud Platform and Security Engineer with nearly 30 years of experience architecting and securing complex cloud-native environments across AWS, Azure, and GCP. My expertise spans Kubernetes, GitOps, and CNCF technologies such as ArgoCD, Crossplane, and OpenTelemetry, enabling enterprises to build scalable, automated, and secure platforms. As a CISSP, CCSP, and SecurityX-certified professional, I focus on integrating strong security principles into every layer of cloud infrastructure. I’m passionate about advancing the CNCF ecosystem, mentoring engineers, and promoting secure, reliable, and compliant cloud adoption worldwide.

everyone.name: Amit Ghosh
everyone.bio: I am a seasoned Devops professional, having expertise in Kubernetes , Service Mesh and in Microservices and Cloud Native development, with vast experience in several major domains like SDK, Security, Public Clouds, CI/CD, Observability . I have a passion for cloud technologies and enjoy exploring CNCF tools

everyone.name: Ansel Stein
everyone.bio: As a DevOps I champion a Cloud Ready approach using GitOps, CI/CD, Security as a first concern, and of course Kubernetes. My focus is always to improve the Developer Experience as too often people forget that as DevOps, our number one clients are developers.

everyone.name: Cailyn Edwards
everyone.bio:
Senior Security Engineer
Kubernetes Org Member

ambassadors.name: Savitha Raghunathan
ambassadors.bio: Savitha Raghunathan is a Senior Software Engineer at Red Hat, currently focusing on Application Modernization and Cloud Native adoption. Her role as a maintainer for the CNCF Sandbox project Konveyor has provided her with valuable insights into the growing demand for modernizing critical applications to align with cloud-native technologies, thereby deepening her understanding of the application lifecycle. In her previous position, Savitha worked as a platform engineer, where she dedicated herself to building internal developer platforms using Kubernetes and other cloud native projects.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Contributing to CNCF projects has been incredibly fulfilling, and the support and positivity I've experienced from the community during difficult periods, including the pandemic, have deepened my bond with this community. This role as a CNCF Ambassador has given me a chance to reciprocate that support, share my valuable experiences, and facilitate the onboarding of new contributors and adopters into the CNCF ecosystem.

ambassadors.name: Robert Sirchia
ambassadors.bio: I create content around CNCF projects, building enablement content around CNCF projects. I help contribute around marketing and community efforts with CNCF projects. I am a new code contributor to a CNCF project

ambassadors.name: Jakub Krzywda
ambassadors.bio: I have over a decade of experience in cloud and distributed systems, and hold a PhD in the field.In the role of Cloud Native Training Manager and Senior Engineer at Elastisys, I am helping organizations adopt cloud native technologies and DevOps practices.As the Linux Foundation Authorized Instructor, I teach all Kubernetes courses in preparation for CKA, CKAD, and CKS exams.
projects: Elastisys Welkin® based on Cluster API

ambassadors.name: Lior Lieberman
ambassadors.bio: With over a decade of experience working on production, large-scale systems, serving million of users, Lior specializes in DevOps and Site Reliability Engineering principles. He has vast experience working with Kubernetes and contributing to the ecosystem, both as a user and a cloud provider. Lior has also been leading Kubernetes networking efforts and is a key contributor to Gateway API. Alongside his technical work, Lior mentors startups and development teams worldwide, providing them advice in R&D growth, platform scalability, cloud deployments and cost efficiency.

ambassadors.name: Kolawole Olowoporoku
ambassadors.bio: I work as a SRE with SEKAI (swiss based startup) prior to joining SEKAI I worked as a Solution Architect with AWS where I worked with startups on several ways of building on AWS. I spend most of my time working across containers, but my philosophy revolves more about buidling cheaply and reliably. I have written several blogs which revolves around this in some way such as, https://thenewstack.io/how-to-run-databases-in-kubernetes/ https://medium.com/@kollzey69/balancing-innovation-and-cost-in-the-cloud-era-rethinking-best-practices-and-the-role-of-3264677ff726 https://techcabal.com/2024/07/29/strategic-tech-choices-a-cost-centric-philosophy-for-ctos/

ambassadors.name: Yuanpeng Liang
ambassadors.bio: Yuanpeng is a kubernetes & cloudnative enthusiast, also is a contributor  of kubernetes and karmada, enjoys understanding and using the projects of cncf and enjoy technology advocacy.

ambassadors.name: Sara Trappetti
ambassadors.bio: I started my journey in Tech Marketing in 2016, and in 2021, I embarked with SIGHUP on the Cloud Native industry as a complete newbie. For the first six months, I felt overwhelmed by the vastness and complexity of the field, but then something amazing happened along the way – thanks to my stubbornness and the patient support of my colleagues, I actively started contributing to the Cloud Native community and found a perfect place to express my skills while helping others, organizing events, and supporting projects - I'm a KCD Italy and Cloud Native Milano Meetup organizer and part of the Glossary Italian Localization Group. All that apart, I'm music-passionate and a restless shower performer, a winter couch knitter and cat cuddler, a balcony gardening ace, a dabble baker, and a great backwarmer during adventurous motorcycle journeys.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a non-tech member and a proud CNCF ambassador, I'm determined to lend my support in any way possible. I'm here to share the lessons I've learned, contribute to the growth of our community, and empower others to discover the Cloud Native Community and find their own path. I want to be a guiding hand to help others navigate CNCF initiatives and projects. It's all about showing that there are different ways to contribute and various subgroups tailored to their expertise.

ambassadors.name: Mahamed Ali
ambassadors.bio: I'm a Senior DevOps Engineer and a maintainer of Kubernetes and Knative. I'm working on improving the cloud infrastructure of the Kubernetes project for our contributors and end-users.  I'm also a member of the Kubestronaut program.

kubestronauts.name: Andrzej (Andy) Kozlowski
kubestronauts.bio: I’m a Lead/Senior Cloud Platform and Security Engineer with nearly 30 years of experience architecting and securing complex cloud-native environments across AWS, Azure, and GCP. My expertise spans Kubernetes, GitOps, and CNCF technologies such as ArgoCD, Crossplane, and OpenTelemetry, enabling enterprises to build scalable, automated, and secure platforms. As a CISSP, CCSP, and SecurityX-certified professional, I focus on integrating strong security principles into every layer of cloud infrastructure. I’m passionate about advancing the CNCF ecosystem, mentoring engineers, and promoting secure, reliable, and compliant cloud adoption worldwide.

kubestronauts.name: Rufat Zakirov
kubestronauts.bio: Experienced Linux/DevOps Administrator proficient in CentOS, Ubuntu, and Red Hat.

kubestronauts.name: Jianfeng Zhang
kubestronauts.bio: I am engaged in cloud computing work and have started learning Kubernetes in the past two years. I hope to gain more knowledge about K8s and meet like-minded friends through CNCF.

kubestronauts.name: Tomáš Kubla
kubestronauts.bio: 🔐 Security specialist who fell in love with cloud-native world.🌍 Currently spreading my roots in the Netherlands — not just in tech, but also in the 🧗 bouldering, 💃 lindy hop, and 👞 tap dance community.💡 Motto: "If describing an issue in an open-source project takes you too longer, just fix it."

goldenKubestronauts.name: Ana Pedra
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Cloud DevOps Engineer | Golden Kubestronaut 🚀 | Hackathon winner 🏆 and lifelong learner with 40+ certifications across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Red Hat, and Kubernetes. Passionate about cloud, Kubernetes, security, and automation — always learning, building, and giving back to the community.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Bandar Hanbashi
goldenKubestronauts.bio: A software engineer passionate about cloud-native technologies and all things automation

goldenKubestronauts.name: Daniel Sulzer
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I'm a DevOps Engineer from Austria, currently based in Switzerland. My focus is on automation, GitOps, Kubernetes and monitoring. I'm always eager to learn new technologies, and the CNCF landscape offers many exciting projects to explore.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Labhesh Hase
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I'm Labhesh Hase, a Kubestronaut passionate about cloud-native security and DevSecOps. I specialize in OCI, AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, and CNCF/cloud security tools like Kyverno, CIS Benchmarks, and Trivy. I help customers across the globe migrate from on-prem to cloud by designing and architecting secure, scalable cloud platforms. I also enjoy long-distance running, using it as a way to stay mentally focused while balancing the intense demands of tech and continuous learning.
